w:r.w:t: Full Project Blueprint • Stack • Architecture • Prompt Ready
Copy and paste this entire prompt into any AI coding assistant (Claude, GPT-4, Cursor, etc.) to start building the project immediately.

You are an expert Python developer specializing in computer vision, AI, and desktop application development. I want you to help me build a complete, production-ready desktop application called "StudySense" — an AI Smart Study Companion that passively monitors a student through their webcam and provides real-time feedback on their focus, emotions, posture, and productivity.
Include this exact text in your prompt to an AI assistant:
PROJECT NAME: StudySense — AI Smart Study Companion
GOAL: A passive background app that monitors student behavior via webcam
and gives real-time intelligent feedback to improve focus & wellbeing.
🔧 Core Modules to Build (include in prompt)
Build a real-time webcam feed processor using OpenCV that:
- Runs MediaPipe FaceMesh (468 landmarks) on each frame
- Runs MediaPipe Pose for body/shoulder detection
- Passes processed data to analysis modules
- Runs in a background thread (never block the UI)
Implement Eye Aspect Ratio (EAR) based fatigue detection:
- Calculate EAR using MediaPipe eye landmarks
- If EAR < 0.25 for 3+ consecutive seconds → fatigue alert
- Track yawn detection via mouth aspect ratio (MAR > 0.6)
- Score fatigue 0-100 and log with timestamp
Integrate DeepFace for real-time emotion detection:
- Sample emotion every 5 seconds (not every frame — too slow)
- Dominant emotions: happy, sad, stressed, neutral, frustrated, tired
- Store emotion timeline in SQLite with Unix timestamp
Build attention scoring using head pose estimation:
- Use MediaPipe FaceMesh to estimate head yaw/pitch/roll
- If head yaw > 30 degrees → looking away
- If head pitch down > 20 degrees → phone checking posture
- Track gaze direction (left/right/center) via iris landmarks
- Trigger alert if focus_score < 60 for 2+ minutes
Use MediaPipe Pose for posture monitoring:
- Extract nose landmark for forward lean detection
- Calculate shoulder tilt angle — alert if > 15 degrees asymmetry
- Calculate forward lean: if nose x-distance from midpoint > threshold
- Alert: 'Straighten your back' if bad posture for 60+ seconds
Use YOLOv8 to detect phone objects in frame:
results = model(frame, classes=[67]) # class 67 = cell phone
- Run YOLO every 2 seconds (not every frame)
Build a rule-based decision engine that generates smart messages:
IF fatigue_score > 70 → 'You seem tired. Take a 5-min break.'
IF emotion == 'sad' for 10+ mins → 'It is okay to feel this way.'
IF distraction_count > 5 in 30 mins → 'Try focus mode.'
IF study_time > 45 mins without break → 'Break recommended.'
IF posture_bad for 60 secs → 'Straighten your back.'
- Pass session_summary to Claude API for personalized message:
Create SQLite database with these tables:
- Use SQLAlchemy ORM for clean database interactions
- All data stays local — NO external uploads
- Real-time status panel: emotion icon, focus %, fatigue bar
- Today's stats: study time, distraction count, breaks taken
Include this in your prompt for the AI coaching feature:
Integrate the Anthropic Claude API as the intelligent coaching layer:
You are a supportive AI study coach. Here is the student's current session data:
Generate ONE short, warm, actionable coaching message (max 15 words).
Be empathetic. Do not repeat generic advice. Context-aware only.
━━━ PROMPT END — COPY EVERYTHING ABOVE THIS LINE ━━━
FLOW: Webcam → OpenCV Frame → MediaPipe (Face + Pose) → Analysis Modules → Decision Engine → PyQt6 UI / Notifications → SQLite Storage
Grabs webcam frames in a background thread at ~12 FPS
Runs MediaPipe FaceMesh — outputs 468 face landmarks + iris points
Runs MediaPipe Pose — outputs 33 body keypoints for posture
Computes EAR + MAR scores, triggers fatigue alerts
Samples DeepFace every 5s, tracks rolling emotion window
Estimates head yaw/pitch, calculates focus % per session
Detects phone in frame every 2 seconds via YOLOv8n
Rule engine + Claude API — generates coaching messages
Reads/writes all session data to local SQLite
PyQt6 dashboard — live stats, charts, alerts
Webcam capture, frame processing, preview rendering
468 face landmarks, eye/mouth/iris tracking in real time
33 body keypoints for shoulder alignment & posture
Plug-and-play emotion labels: happy, sad, stressed, etc.
Fast phone detection in background, runs every 2 seconds
Generates personalized coaching messages from session data
Speech-to-text for 'How am I doing?' voice commands
Native app window, system tray, notifications
Interactive mood trends, focus time, distraction heatmaps
100% local storage, all session history, no cloud needed
Random Forest trained on weekly data to predict burnout risk
Cross-platform desktop alerts and system tray icon
Webcam capture + MediaPipe face mesh + live preview window
EAR calculation, eye closure detection, yawn detection alert
Head pose estimation, gaze tracking, focus score calculation
Shoulder landmark tracking, tilt alert, forward lean detection
YOLOv8n integration, distraction counter, event logging
Database setup, session logging, event storage
Main window, live stats panel, system tray, notifications
API integration, dynamic message generation, voice output
Plotly charts, mood trends, weekly focus stats
scikit-learn model, weekly pattern analysis, risk score
PyInstaller build, settings UI, onboarding screen
Use these focused prompts to build each module individually with any AI assistant.
Prompt A — Start the Vision Pipeline

"Build a Python webcam processing class using OpenCV and MediaPipe. The class should run in a background thread, capture frames at 12 FPS, run MediaPipe FaceMesh and Pose on each frame, and expose the latest landmarks via a thread-safe property. Include proper start/stop controls."

"Write a Python function that takes MediaPipe face landmarks and calculates the Eye Aspect Ratio (EAR) for both eyes. If the EAR stays below 0.25 for more than 3 seconds, return a fatigue alert. Also detect yawning via Mouth Aspect Ratio (MAR > 0.6). Return a fatigue_score from 0-100."

"Integrate DeepFace into a Python class that analyzes emotion every 5 seconds from an OpenCV frame. Track the rolling emotion over a 60-second window and return the dominant emotion and confidence score. Handle cases where no face is detected gracefully."

"Build a PyQt6 desktop application with: a main dashboard showing real-time emotion icon, focus percentage bar, fatigue level, and today's study stats. Include a system tray icon using pystray that minimizes the window, and a notification system using plyer. Use a dark blue color scheme."

"Write a Python function that takes a study session summary dict (emotion_trend, focus_score, fatigue_level, distraction_count, study_minutes, posture_status) and calls the Anthropic Claude API to generate a single short, warm, context-aware coaching message of maximum 15 words. Use claude-opus-4-6 model."

"Using Plotly and PyQt6, build an analytics tab that displays: (1) a weekly bar chart of daily focus time, (2) a line chart of mood trends over 7 days, (3) a scatter plot showing distraction events over time, and (4) a productivity score card. Pull all data from SQLite using SQLAlchemy."
⚠️ Include these requirements in your prompt to ensure ethical implementation:
All processing must be done 100% locally on the device — no video or data uploaded to any cloud service
The webcam indicator LED must be visible whenever monitoring is active
User must give explicit consent before the app starts monitoring for the first time
A pause/stop button must always be accessible in the system tray and main window
All stored session data must be user-deletable from within the settings panel
No raw video frames should ever be saved to disk — only derived statistics
Claude API calls should only send anonymized session statistics, never images or video
The app must display a clear privacy policy on first launch
StudySense — Built with Python, OpenCV, MediaPipe, DeepFace, YOLOv8, Claude API, PyQt6, SQLite | All data stays local | 2025
